title: Enhanced Oil Recovery (EOR) Modeling
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Enhanced Oil Recovery (EOR) Modeling: EOR simulation: model waterflooding, CO2 miscible, or polymer flooding to predict incremental recovery factor. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.fractional_flow.buckley_leverett…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~500; viscous_fingering_onset, gravity_override_degree set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: dS_w/dt + df_w/dx = 0 (Buckley-Leverett); for CO2: compositional with MMP; for polymer: shear-thinning viscosity model
E.world_state_x: saturation S_w(x,t) and composition z_i(x,t) after EOR injection
E.observation_y: production data {q_o, q_w, tracers}(t), incremental recovery factor RF

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D_saturation_composition is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 500); viscous_fingering_onset dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Measurement gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: RF prediction within 3% of observed. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~500. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |viscous_fingering_onset|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_cells * N_timesteps) for displacement calculation per iteration
